hq: [Company Address] — not disclosed in report; placeholder text present

_flag: NEAR-DUPLICATE OF DOC 1 (1oQCKQkwionDFbDSvK8IbzDBGizykStb9wfticXu2xXM). This is another Delve SOC 2 Type 2 / Azure report for the same period (Jan 1 - Apr 1, 2025) by Accorp Partners. Same template, same placeholder issues. Minor structural differences: 85 pages vs 81 pages in DOC 1; antivirus definitions updated daily (vs weekly in DOC 1). Trust scope is Security only (Processing Integrity and Privacy explicitly excluded as 'not relevant'). DOC 1 listed trust_criteria as Security + Availability + Confidentiality — this report's auditor scope letter mentions Security only. The org chart on p.21 shows same AI/ML Team, Product Team, Engineering Team, Growth Team, Customer Success Team, Sales Department structure.

system_description.overview: Delve Technologies Inc. provides an AI-automated compliance platform designed to help companies achieve and maintain compliance with frameworks including SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, ISO 27001, and PCI DSS. The platform leverages AI to automate evidence gathering, perform continuous compliance monitoring, and includes AI code scanning, infrastructure monitoring, automated security questionnaire completion, and access control management.

products.Automated Compliance Management Platform: End-to-end compliance automation for SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO 27001, GDPR, PCI DSS, and more; integration with client existing tech stack; continuous compliance monitoring and gap identification; automated evidence collection.
products.AI-Powered Security and Compliance Featu: Automated code scanning (review of code pushes for security vulnerabilities), automated infrastructure scanning (continuous monitoring), access control monitoring (detection and alerts for unauthorized access), security questionnaire automation.
products.Compliance Support Services: Compliance implementation services including onboarding, setup, and framework implementation; ongoing compliance support including expert guidance and auditor coordination.

infrastructure.cloud_provider: Microsoft Azure (carve-out — Azure data center controls excluded from scope)
regions: Not specified — Azure regions not named in report
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical offices; all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ — report states 'high availability data centers with multiple availability zones'; DR plan references spinning up multiple servers across all availability zones in Azure

network_architecture.network_segmentation: System hosted in Microsoft Azure in a virtual private cloud (VPC) environment with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS deployed via Azure to identify and protect against threats.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through a bastion host with a virtual private network (VPN). VPC VPN connections shown in network diagram.
network_architecture.tls: TLS with certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Internet -> VPN/Internet Gateway -> VPC WEST and VPC EAST (multiple availability zones) with private subnets. VPC VPN Gateways connect via IPSec VPN tunnel. Prod HA, Dev, Test, D.com in VPC WEST; Prod HR in VPC EAST. Remote users connect via bastion host + VPN. NOTE: Same placeholder network diagram as other Delve reports — instruction text overlay present.

compute: Azure virtual servers (cloud architecture including database, networking devices, virtual servers)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development, test, staging, and production environments. Changes tested in separate development or test environment before implementation.

databases: Not named — 'cloud architecture including database' mentioned generically
data_storage.backup_frequency: Daily (implied by multi-AZ backup configuration; not explicitly stated in this report variant — confirmed in DOC 1 as daily)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Azure Active Directory (passwords controlled through Password policy of Azure; Active Directory referenced for server and database accounts)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required for source code tool access; MFA for email
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Azure password policy: periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts; guest and anonymous logins not allowed; unattended desktops locked on inactivity; biometrics as unlock alternative

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: Azure KMS (cloud provider key management service) — access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named — 'source code management tool' referenced with MFA access
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to production requires additional approval. Developers cannot make changes to production without additional approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as code; configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management set up automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named — 'monitoring tool' that generates alerts on predefined thresholds
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named — logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions, and roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure / data center services (subservice organization, carve-out)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed on all production servers (continuous scanning, daily definition updates per p.20) and all desktops/laptops (daily updates from vendor website). NOTE: This report says 'daily' for AV definition updates; DOC 1 said 'weekly' for production servers.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; internal IT Infrastructure team performs periodic scans
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annually by independent third-party; high/critical findings remediated in timely manner
security_tools.cybersecurity_insurance: Not explicitly mentioned in this report variant (present in DOC 1)

compliance_controls.total_controls_tested: Not verified — ToC shows test results section starts p.59; report is 85 pages (4 more than DOC 1's 81 pages). Likely similar count to DOC 1's 57 controls.
untestable: Not verified in this reading — likely similar to DOC 1
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Azure responsible for physical security of data center
A1.2 — Delve does not have an office; environmental security managed by building management/Azure
Processing Integrity criteria — deemed not relevant to system
Privacy criteria — deemed not relevant to system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2 — Branch protection rules enforced; merge requires additional approval
CC8.1.3 — Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed in SCM tool with MFA access
CC4.1.1 — Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC6.1.5 — Cloud provider KMS for encryption key management

infrastructure.rationale: Same as DOC 1: Azure multi-AZ confirmed, VPC with IDS/IPS, VPN, daily backups. No named regions, no CDN, no multi-region. Same placeholder network diagram. Single cloud provider.

application_architecture.rationale: Separate environments confirmed, branch protection confirmed, IaC referenced. No languages, frameworks, compute types, API style, or CI/CD tool disclosed.

data_layer.rationale: Daily backups confirmed (implied), multi-AZ confirmed, data classification and retention policies confirmed, per-tenant segregation confirmed. No specific database technologies named.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS, firewalls, MFA enforced, anti-virus/malware, quarterly access reviews, 1-business-day termination SLA, KMS encryption. No WAF, no SIEM. Cybersecurity insurance not explicitly mentioned in this variant.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC referenced, branch protection confirmed, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch management. No CI/CD tool named, no SCM platform named, no container orchestration.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, annual review and testing confirmed, daily backups confirmed, multi-AZ failover confirmed. No RTO/RPO disclosed. No multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only Azure explicitly named as subservice organization. No other third-party vendors disclosed. Single critical cloud dependency.

overall.rationale: Substantively identical to DOC 1. Same template placeholder issues (network diagram, signing authority 'Your Name Here', company address). SOC 2 Type 2 with Azure. 85-page report vs 81 pages in DOC 1 — the extra pages likely reflect additional controls or test details. Trust scope is Security only (same as effective scope in DOC 1 after exclusions). Same assessment: solid foundational controls but very limited tech specificity and critical template completion failures.

red_flags: CRITICAL: This report appears to be a near-duplicate of DOC 1 (1oQCKQkwionDFbDSvK8IbzDBGizykStb9wfticXu2xXM) — same company, same period, same auditor, same cloud provider. Having two nearly identical SOC 2 Type 2 reports for the same period is unusual and unexplained.
CRITICAL: Network diagram is explicitly a placeholder/sample — same instruction overlay text as DOC 1.
CRITICAL: Multiple highlighted instruction blocks remain in the report body — partially-completed Delve template used as their own report.
Signing authority name field is 'Your Name Here' — placeholder not completed.
Only 3-month audit period (Jan-Apr 2025) — shorter than typical 12-month SOC 2 Type 2.
yellow_flags: Unclear why two nearly identical Azure SOC 2 Type 2 reports exist for the same period — may be different template versions or different customer-facing variants.
No CI/CD tool named despite source code management being referenced.
No SIEM or log aggregation platform named.
Audit firm 'Accorp Partners' — less established than Big 4 or major SOC auditors.
Cybersecurity insurance not explicitly mentioned (present in DOC 1).
AV definition update frequency stated as 'daily' in this report vs 'weekly' in DOC 1 — inconsistency between template versions.
green_flags: Zero exceptions across all tested controls.
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party.
Multi-AZ deployment with daily backups confirmed.
MFA enforced for administrative and production access.
1-business-day access revocation SLA for terminated employees.
IaC used for infrastructure management with rollback capability.
Branch protection rules enforced with required approvals for production merges.
BCDR plan with annual testing confirmed.
key_observations: This is a second Delve Azure SOC 2 Type 2 report covering the same period (Jan 1 - Apr 1, 2025) as DOC 1. The existence of two near-identical reports suggests Delve generates multiple template variants — possibly one per customer, or this represents different template versions of their compliance platform output.
This report is 85 pages vs DOC 1's 81 pages — 4 additional pages likely in the controls/test results section.
Trust criteria in scope: Security only (Processing Integrity and Privacy explicitly excluded as 'not relevant'; CC6.4 and A1.2 excluded citing Azure).
AV definition update frequency is stated as 'daily' on p.20 (vs 'weekly for production servers' in DOC 1) — this inconsistency between template variants is a yellow flag for controls accuracy.
The org chart on p.21 explicitly shows an AI/ML Team alongside Product, Engineering, Growth, Customer Success, and Sales teams — confirming Delve has dedicated AI/ML engineering capacity.
Management assertion date is 8/7/2025 for a Jan-Apr 2025 period — same 4-month lag as DOC 1.
Same PAC-FIRM-LIC-47383 auditor license number as DOC 1.
